title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nasal congestion
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nasal congestion are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, created by soaking plant material in a mixture of water and alcohol. These tinctures can provide relief from nasal congestion by reducing inflammation, thinning mucus, and easing breathing. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with nasal congestion include elderberry, goldenseal, and eucalyptus. By incorporating these tinctures into daily routines, individuals can alleviate the discomfort and inconvenience caused by nasal congestion, improving their overall quality of life. For instance, someone suffering from allergies may use goldenseal tincture to minimize symptoms, allowing them to enjoy outdoor activities and improve their focus at work.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with nasal congestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce swelling in the nasal passages and fight off viruses that cause congestion. Additionally, yarrow is known to stimulate the production of sweat, which can help to eliminate toxins and reduce fevers, further aiding in the relief of nasal congestion. The astringent properties of yarrow also help to tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing the amount of mucus produced and providing quick relief from a stuffy nose. Overall, yarrow tincture is a natural and effective way to alleviate nasal congestion and promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which is important for nasal congestion as inflammation can narrow and constrict the airways. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture has the ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, which is important for nasal congestion as it can help prevent or treat infections that can cause or worsen congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture can help tighten and dry up mucous membranes, which is important for nasal congestion as it can help reduce the production of excess mucus. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture can help alleviate pain and discomfort, which is important for nasal congestion as it can help relieve the pain and pressure associated with congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids and volatile oils.

Diaphoretic: Yarrow tincture can help promote sweating, which is important for nasal congestion as it can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever, which can contribute to congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal inflammation and congestion.
Leaves: Contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been shown to have decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Possess similar chemical constituents as leaves, providing similar decongestant and anti-inflammatory benefits for nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers, washing them gently with clean water to remove dirt.
Chop the fresh yarrow material into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears, measuring out 250g.
Combine the chopped yarrow with 80% proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it three-quarters full with the mixture.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tincture helps with nasal congestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, garlic has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help fight off infections that may be causing the congestion. Its potent compounds, such as allicin, work to stimulate the immune system, providing further relief from nasal congestion. Overall, garlic tincture can help alleviate nasal congestion and promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garlic tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. This is due to the presence of diallyl disulfide and other organosulfur compounds.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties fight off infections that can cause nasal congestion, thanks to allicin and ajoene.
Expectorant: Garlic tincture aids in breaking up and expelling mucus, providing relief from congestion. This is attributed to allicin and S-allyl cysteine.
Immune-boosting: By strengthening the immune system, garlic tincture helps the body fight off the causes of nasal congestion. This is mainly due to allicin and selenium.
Vasodilatory: Garlic tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels can help increase airflow and reduce congestion. This is attributed to nitric oxide and hydrogen sulfide.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are often used to make medicinal tinctures for nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the garlic plant have been used in tinctures to help clear nasal congestion, as they contain natural oils with decongestant and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Garlic flowers contain essential oils and allicin, which have been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in tinctures for nasal congestion.

Seeds: Garlic seeds, also known as garlic cloves, are rich in allicin and other compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures for nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic cloves from your garden or local farmer's market weighing approximately one pound.
Peel the garlic cloves and chop them into small pieces to release their active compounds and oils.
Combine the chopped garlic with 80 proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar covering the garlic completely.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the mixture to infuse and extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids after four hours of pressing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden angelica tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. This herbaceous plant, also known as Archangelica officinalis, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, and studies suggest that its active compounds, such as alpha-pinene and limonene, may help alleviate nasal congestion by relaxing the smooth muscles in the respiratory tract. Angelica tincture can provide natural relief for congested nasal passages, promoting easier breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion by constricted blood vessels and swelling in the nasal lining. Active constituents include essential oils and coumarins.
Expectorant: The tincture helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion. This is due to the presence of essential oils and organic acids.

Antimicrobial: Angelica tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat infections that can cause nasal congestion. Active constituents include essential oils, coumarins, and organic acids.
Diaphoretic: The tincture promotes sweating, which can help to reduce congestion by opening up the nasal passages. This is due to the presence of essential oils and coumarins.
Analgesic: Angelica tincture alleviates pain, providing relief from the discomfort associated with nasal congestion. Active constituents include essential oils, coumarins, and organic acids.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of garden angelica are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Seeds: The seeds of garden angelica contain essential oils that have been found to have decongestant and antiseptic properties, making them useful for nasal congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of garden angelica have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, including nasal congestion, due to their expectorant and soothing properties.

Flowers: The flowers of garden angelica contain compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating nasal congestion caused by inflammation or infection.
Stems: The stems of garden angelica contain essential oils that have been found to have expectorant and decongestant properties, making them useful for treating nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica leaves and stems in the early morning, choosing only healthy, vibrant growth.
Chop 100g of harvested plant material into small pieces, then combine with 250ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for at least 4 weeks to allow the tincture to macerate and extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the clear tincture.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark-glass bottles, labeling and storing in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years with no degradation of potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with nasal congestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The herb contains compounds such as thujone and alpha-thujone, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and relieve congestion. Additionally, wormwood has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help to break up mucus and make it easier to expel. This makes wormwood tincture a valuable natural remedy for those suffering from nasal congestion due to colds, flu, or allergies. Its ability to soothe irritated nasal tissues and promote healthy breathing makes it a popular choice among those seeking a more natural approach to managing nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which is important for nasal congestion as inflammation can cause swelling and blockage in the nasal passages, and the active constituents responsible for this property include beta-myrcene, camphene, and limonene.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tincture has the ability to kill or inhibit the growth of microorganisms that can cause nasal congestion, and the active constituents responsible for this property include thujone, artemisinin, and cineole.

Expectorant: Wormwood tincture helps to expel mucus from the respiratory tract, which is important for nasal congestion as excess mucus can contribute to blockage and difficulty breathing, and the active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Analgesic: Wormwood tincture can help to relieve pain associated with nasal congestion, including headaches and sinus pressure, and the active constituents responsible for this property include thujone, camphene, and beta-pinene.

Vasodilator: Wormwood tincture can help to widen blood vessels and improve circulation in the nasal passages, which is important for nasal congestion as it can help to reduce swelling and improve breathing, and the active constituents responsible for this property include chamazulene and bisabolol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain volatile oils, including camphor and eucalyptol, which have decongestant and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce swelling and fight infection.
Seeds: High in thujone, a compound with strong decongestant and stimulant effects, which helps clear nasal passages.
Bark: Contains bitter compounds that stimulate the production of mucus and have a soothing effect on inflamed mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and stems of optimal potency for best results.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its essential oils quickly.
Combine the cut plant material with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for at least 14 days and up to 28 days slowly.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian frankincense tincture helps with nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The main active compound in frankincense, boswellic acid, helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion. Additionally, frankincense tincture can help loosen and expel mucus, making it easier to breathe. Its soothing properties can also help alleviate other symptoms associated with nasal congestion, such as sinus pressure and headaches. Overall, the herbal tincture can provide natural and effective relief for nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Indian frankincense tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to boswellic acids, help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.
Expectorant: The expectorant effect of Indian frankincense tincture, attributed to alpha-thujene, assists in thinning and clearing mucus, providing relief from congestion.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting properties of Indian frankincense tincture, from monoterpenes like limonene, help strengthen the body's defenses against respiratory infections causing nasal congestion.
Analgesic: Indian frankincense tincture's analgesic properties, due to incensole acetate, help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with nasal congestion.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Indian frankincense tincture, from compounds like linalool, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Resin: The resin, also known as olibanum, contains boswellic acids which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve nasal congestion.
Buds: The buds contain a higher concentration of essential oils, which have expectorant and decongestant properties, making them useful for treating nasal congestion.
Stems: The stems contain compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and mucus production, helping to alleviate nasal congestion.
Leaves: The leaves contain monoterpenes, which have been found to have antimicrobial and expectorant properties, useful for treating nasal congestion.
Seeds: The seeds contain a volatile oil that has been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, including nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata resin in the morning when it's most potent.
Dry the harvested resin at room temperature for several weeks or use a desiccant.
Grind 20-30 grams of dried resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered resin with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar, then seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which can help boost the immune system and reduce congestion caused by cold and flu symptoms. The tincture form allows for faster absorption into the bloodstream, providing quicker relief from nasal congestion. Overall, herbal bell pepper tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for those seeking relief from stuffy noses and congested sinuses.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Vasodilatory: The tincture can help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing congestion, due to the presence of capsaicin.
Antioxidant: Bell pepper tincture can protect against damage from free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting respiratory health, due to the high levels of vitamin C and other antioxidants.
Decongestant: The tincture can help to shrink swollen nasal tissues, reducing congestion, due to the presence of volatile oils.
Expectorant: Bell pepper tincture can help to thin and expel mucus, reducing congestion, thanks to the presence of vitamin C and other compounds.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, bell pepper flowers can help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages.
Leaves: High in essential oils, bell pepper leaves can help clear nasal congestion by acting as a natural decongestant and expectorant.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and bioactive compounds, bell pepper seeds can provide relief from nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and acting as a natural antihistamine.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bell peppers selecting sweet or slightly hot varieties for flavor and aroma preservation.
Clean and chop the peppers into small pieces removing seeds and membranes to enhance bioavailability.
Combine one part chopped bell peppers with two parts 190 proof vodka in a clean glass jar allowing steeping.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking daily to facilitate solvent extraction processes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter pressing gently to extract herbal essence and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains eucalyptus oil, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the menthol present in the blue gum leaves can help to alleviate congestion by providing a cooling sensation and helping to open up the airways. Overall, the combination of these compounds in the tincture can provide relief for nasal congestion and promote easier breathing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion by acting on the inflamed tissues. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily the eucalyptus oil and the flavonoids found in the blue gum leaves.

Expectorant: This tincture helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, which is important for nasal congestion as it promotes the clearing of blocked nasal passages. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is eucalyptus oil.

Decongestant: Tasmanian blue gum tincture has a decongestant effect, which helps to narrow the blood vessels in the lining of the nose, reducing swelling and easing breathing. The active constituent responsible for this property is eucalyptus oil.

Antimicrobial: This tincture has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off infections, which can contribute to nasal congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily the eucalyptus oil and the tannins found in the blue gum leaves.

Analgesic: Tasmanian blue gum tincture has analgesic properties that help to relieve pain, which is important for nasal congestion as it can cause discomfort and headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily the eucalyptus oil and the flavonoids found in the blue gum leaves.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain eucalyptus oil, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve nasal congestion.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, aiding in clearing nasal passages.
Bark: Has tannins and essential oils, known for their astringent and decongestant properties, useful in treating nasal congestion.
Buds: Contain high amounts of eucalyptus oil, providing relief from nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting mucus expulsion.
Seeds: Source of essential oils, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, assisting in clearing blocked nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tasmanian blue gum leaves in early morning dew for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or dehydrator at low temperature for 6 hours.
Grind 500g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and store in an airtight container.
Mix 1 part powdered tasmanian blue gum with 2 parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar, allowing for at least 30 days of maceration time.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding solids and preserving the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient that has been shown to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. This herbal remedy can help to alleviate nasal congestion by reducing swelling in the nasal passages and promoting healthy mucus flow. Additionally, echinacea has been found to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing nasal congestion. By using eastern purple coneflower tincture, individuals may be able to find relief from nasal congestion and breathe easier.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which is important for nasal congestion as inflammation can cause swelling and blockage in the nasal passages. The active constituents responsible for this property include chlorogenic acid, caffeic acid, and luteolin.

Antimicrobial: The tincture has the ability to fight off microorganisms causing infections, which is crucial for nasal congestion as infections can lead to further inflammation and blockage. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones, such as eudesmanolides and germacranolides.

Analgesic: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps alleviate pain, which is important for nasal congestion as congestion can cause discomfort and headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin.

Expectorant: The tincture aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, which is important for nasal congestion as mucus buildup can worsen congestion and make breathing difficult. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyacetylenes and volatile oils.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tincture supports the immune system, which is crucial for nasal congestion as a strong immune system can help the body fight off infections and reduce inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides, such as inulin and arabinogalactan.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils and antioxidants, making the leaves useful for creating decongestant and anti-inflammatory tinctures.
Buds: High in phenolic compounds, buds can provide relief from nasal congestion by reducing swelling in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds can help alleviate nasal congestion by supporting respiratory health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers, ensuring the plant material is free from contamination.
Cleanse the plant material by gently shaking off excess dirt and debris, then air-drying it for 24 hours to prevent moisture retention.
Grind 100g of cleaned plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, preserving as much essential oil as possible.
Combine the powdered plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a dark glass container, stirring well to ensure thorough saturation and then sealing tightly for 4-6 weeks' maceration time.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture at room temperature for up to 2 years' shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to stimulate the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that helps dilate blood vessels and improve airflow in the nasal passages. This makes it an effective natural remedy for managing nasal congestion and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Siberian ginseng tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion by limiting swelling and mucus production. This property is due to the presence of eleutherosides and saponins.

Immune system support: Siberian ginseng tincture boosts the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can cause nasal congestion. The active constituents responsible for this effect are eleutherosides and polysaccharides.

Antioxidant properties: Siberian ginseng tincture contains antioxidants that protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nasal congestion. The antioxidant properties come from phenolic compounds found in the tincture.

Adaptogenic properties: Siberian ginseng tincture helps the body adapt to stress, which can reduce the severity of nasal congestion caused by stress-related factors. Eleutherosides are the active constituents responsible for this adaptogenic effect.

Decongestant properties: Siberian ginseng tincture can help relieve nasal congestion by acting as a natural decongestant. This property is due to the presence of essential oils and phenolic compounds found in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain the highest concentration of active compounds, making them the most commonly used part for treating nasal congestion.
Rhizomes: Rich in medicinal properties, they help alleviate congestion by reducing inflammation and strengthening the immune system.
Seeds: Used in tinctures to help clear nasal passages due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of eleutherococcus senticosus in quantities of one pound per batch.
Cut the roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one part powdered root with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four to six weeks, shaking the container daily during the first week.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang tincture helps with nasal congestion because it contains ephedrine, a naturally occurring alkaloid that acts as a decongestant. Ephedrine constricts the blood vessels in the lining of the nose, reducing swelling and inflammation, and helping to clear the airways. This action helps to alleviate nasal congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ma huang tincture has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and loosen mucus, further easing congestion and promoting clearer breathing. Overall, ma huang tincture is a valuable herbal remedy for treating nasal congestion and promoting respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Ma Huang tincture contains ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which act as potent vasoconstrictors, shrinking swollen blood vessels in the nasal passages and reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory: The active constituents, such as essential oils and flavonoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, alleviating inflammation in the nasal passages and easing breathing.
Expectorant: Ma Huang tincture stimulates the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear nasal congestion and relieve symptoms associated with sinus pressure.

Bronchodilator: Ephedrine and other alkaloids in Ma Huang tincture relax bronchial smooth muscles, widening the airways and improving airflow, which can help with nasal congestion caused by asthma or allergies.

Immune system support: Ma Huang tincture contains vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that support the immune system, which may help the body fight off infections and reduce the severity and duration of nasal congestion caused by colds or flu.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of the ma huang plant contain ephedrine, a natural decongestant that helps to alleviate nasal congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of the ma huang plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers of the ma huang plant contain phenolic acids and other compounds that have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
Seeds: The seeds of the ma huang plant contain essential fatty acids and other compounds that can help soothe irritated nasal passages and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ephedra sinica roots and stems in their entirety or as cut material.
Clean and chop the ephedra sinica material into small pieces weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the chopped ephedra sinica with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:4.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 14 to 21 days shaking the jar daily to facilitate maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nasal congestion with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and expectorant herbs. Goldenseal and eucalyptus are popular choices for their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. Meanwhile, licorice and ginger have expectorant effects that help loosen and expel mucus. Mix 20-30 drops of these tinctures in a glass of water, and take it up to three times a day. Always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nasal congestion that are treated with herbal tinctures include sinusitis and allergic rhinitis. For sinusitis, tinctures made from goldenseal, echinacea, and yarrow may be used to reduce inflammation and fight infection. For allergic rhinitis, tinctures made from butterbur and nettle may be used to alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, itching, and runny nose. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures to ensure safety and effectiveness.
